title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings for the Red Sox, striking out seven and allowing one hit against the Yankees.
Tolle maintained a perfect game bid into the sixth inning before conceding a hit, earning a standing ovation at Fenway Park.
Battling illness, Tolle has a 2.78 ERA over 12 starts this season, enhancing his reputation as a formidable starter.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Celtics are exploring improvements while keeping Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum together amid trade rumors.
Potential moves include trading Derrick White for Trey Murphy and exploring center upgrades like Mitchell Robinson or Zach Collins.
Further strategies involve offloading Sam Hauser's contract and considering LeBron James at a veteran discount.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins acquired JJ Peterka from Utah Mammoth for two first-round picks, including Florida's conditional 2028 pick.
Peterka, 24, is known for his speed and scoring ability, averaging 26 goals per season over the last three years.
German-born JJ Peterka struggled in Utah, experiencing reduced ice time and scoring, but can boost his stats with more power-play time in Boston.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics are fielding trade offers for Jaylen Brown; Portland and Detroit are reportedly interested.
Portland Trailblazers could offer a mix of young players and draft picks for Jaylen Brown.
Detroit Pistons may consider a sign-and-trade deal involving center Jalen Duren for Jaylen Brown.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: James Cameron's 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' is now available on Disney+ with Oona Chaplin as Varang.
David Corenswet stars in 'Superman,' streaming on HBO Max, a relatable take on the iconic superhero.
Netflix streams 'Avatar: The Last Airbender' Season 2, continuing Aang's journey in the live-action series.

title: Red Sox place Marcelo Mayer on 10-day IL with forearm issue
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Marcelo Mayer placed on 10-day IL by Red Sox due to forearm issue, severity uncertain.
Tsung-Che Cheng activated from Worcester to play shortstop for Boston against Yankees.
Red Sox struggling with multiple injuries, impacting key players like Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet.

title: Bruins reportedly trade for Utah forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are trading two first-round picks to acquire Utah Mammoth forward JJ Peterka.
Peterka, 24, has a $7.7 million cap hit and a contract through the 2029-30 season.
Peterka scored 25 goals and 22 assists in the 2025-26 season with the Utah Mammoth.

title: Craig Breslow on his job security, whether the Red Sox will buy or sell, and more
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Craig Breslow, Red Sox chief baseball officer, remains optimistic despite team's last-place standing in the AL East.
Breslow emphasized realistic assessment of team's performance as trade deadline approaches, with possible buying or selling decisions.
Job security for Breslow isn't discussed with owner John Henry; focus remains on team improvement and playoff contention.

title: Review: ‘Supergirl’ is a hero in search of a better movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Milly Alcock stars as Kara Zor-El in 'Supergirl,' portraying a party-loving heroine with a heart for justice.
Directed by Craig Gillespie, 'Supergirl' offers a blend of action and humor, though it lacks originality.
Supporting characters include Eve Ridley as Ruthye and Jason Momoa as Lobo, introducing future DC film spinoffs.

title: One of Cambridge’s most beloved Japanese restaurants is closing after 34 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Tampopo in Cambridge closes after 34 years, owner Yasumasa Ito to retire.
Located at 1815 Massachusetts Ave., Tampopo to serve final meal June 30.
Customers and former staff share memories, Kotaro Morita returns from Japan to help.
